title: Dandeli National Park
tagline: Dandeli National Park — wildlife reserve of Karnataka

overview.short: Dandeli National Park is a protected area in the Western Ghats, India.
overview.description: Dandeli National Park is a protected area in the Western Ghats, India. The park was created in 2007 by merging with the neighboring Anshi Nature Reserve as part of a tiger protection program. Today, they cover an area of about 834 square kilometers. Nature lovers here can enjoy an unforgettable landscape and admire the richness of diversity.

topPlaces.name: Kali Tiger Reserve
topPlaces.description: Kali Tiger Reserve (formerly Dandeli-Anshi Tiger Reserve) is a protected area and tiger reserve. It is located in Uttara Kannada district, in Karnataka, India.

items.note: Kali Tiger Reserve (formerly Dandeli-Anshi Tiger Reserve) is a protected area and tiger reserve. It is located in Uttara Kannada district, in Karnataka, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dandeli National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dandeli National Park is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dandeli National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Goa International Airport, Dabolim (71 km) and the nearest railway station is Madgaon Junction (Goa) (53 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dandeli National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dandeli National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dandeli National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Dandeli National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dandeli National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kali Tiger Reserve.
